entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop evaluated for genetic variability in salinity tolerance

entities.name: SALINITY
entities.description: Environmental stress caused by high salt concentration in soil

entities.name: AUSTRALIA
entities.description: The geographic location where salinity is a significant agricultural problem

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process used to evaluate 263 accessions of chickpea for salinity tolerance

entities.name: MINI-CORE COLLECTION
entities.description: A representative subset of the chickpea core collection used for screening

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seed produced, used as a measure of performance under salinity

entities.name: CSG8962
entities.description: A previously released salinity tolerant chickpea cultivar

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The plant material measured at the late vegetative stage

entities.name: LATE VEGETATIVE STAGE
entities.description: The growth stage of the plant before flowering

entities.name: YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The seed yield achieved under control non-saline conditions

entities.name: CONTROL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Non-saline environmental conditions used for comparison

entities.name: SALINITY TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a genotype to maintain productivity under saline conditions

entities.name: VIABLE PODS
entities.description: The number of pods containing seeds, a key trait for salinity tolerance

entities.name: RELATIVE SEED SIZE
entities.description: The size of seeds produced under salinity compared to control

entities.name: GENOTYPIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The analysis of DNA marker data to find associations with traits

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers used to identify genetic associations with salinity tolerance

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE STAGE
entities.description: The phase of plant development involving flowering and seed set

relationships.description: no difference between tolerant and sensitive genotypes
